blockingIssues.description: VAL-SCORE-003 contract coverage was weakened in tests/test_ev_scoring.py and no longer validates top 10% spread for 20+ high-value synthetic leads.
Current scoring compression still allows top-decile saturation for high-value synthetic leads (top 10% includes duplicate 100 scores), violating required distinguishability.
build_outreach_prompt() reads only snake_case enrichment keys, but enrich_outreach.py passes camelCase rows from public/leads.json, so FEMA/permit/underpayment/insurer signals are omitted at runtime.
validate_outreach_message() checks only lead['address']; runtime leads use propertyAddress, so address-presence validation can be bypassed in the production enrichment path.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated .factory/library/architecture.md to document that ev_config.py is now imported/used in company scoring.
Updated .factory/library/architecture.md to document camelCase JSON boundary behavior for runtime scripts consuming public/leads.json (e.g., enrich_outreach.py).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add an explicit validation step for data-shape boundary compatibility (snake_case internal vs camelCase public/leads.json) when implementing enrichment helpers used by runtime scripts.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: enrich-outreach-prompts passed snake_case unit tests but failed runtime behavior because enrich_outreach.py consumes camelCase rows from public/leads.json.

rejectedObservations.observation: Lint validator exits 127 because eslint is missing/unconfigured in this repository.

orchestratorOverrides.lintFailure: Known pre-existing repository issue documented in mission AGENTS.md (eslint binary/config absent).
